description: Machine-readable mapping of coordination surfaces across Ethereum based on interpretive domain grouping.

surfaces.name: Order Flow Access & Routing
surfaces.description: Access to transaction flow, routing mechanisms, and user intent entry points.

surfaces.name: Solver Coordination
surfaces.description: Coordination of execution strategies and intent resolution across competing solvers.

surfaces.name: Builder Coordination
surfaces.description: Coordination of payload construction and proposer-builder interaction, transitioning from market-based to protocol-defined roles.

surfaces.name: Inclusion
surfaces.description: Constraint mechanisms ensuring transaction inclusion and censorship resistance.

surfaces.name: Commitments & Preconfirmations
surfaces.description: Coordination of early execution guarantees and commitment signaling prior to final inclusion.

surfaces.name: Finality
surfaces.description: Consensus-level confirmation and irreversibility of state transitions.

ambiguous_surfaces.name: Execution Coordination (Ambiguous)
ambiguous_surfaces.description: Non-canonical coordination surface spanning routing, solver activity, execution strategy, and payload construction.

notes: Surfaces are interpretive groupings, not protocol-defined layers.
Surfaces may overlap and interact dynamically.
Some surfaces represent transitional abstractions rather than stable primitives.
Execution coordination is explicitly tracked as an ambiguous and non-canonical surface.
